name: Waste c-Si PV panel, takeback and recycling; at treatment plant
description:
This cradle-to-gate unit process dataset is for the treatment of 1 kg of crystalline silicon photovoltaic panels.
This unit process includes transport; production and use of electricity; disposal of process waste; recovery of used products via reuse and recycling.
category: 56: Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services/5622: Waste Treatment and Disposal

processDocumentation.timeDescription: The period spans from beginning of the collection period from Wambach et al. (2017) to the publication date of Frischknecht et al. (2020), since the model has remained valid throughout this period.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: The original inventory from Wambach et al. (2017) was based on one Belgian plant, two Italian, and two German. Germany is selected as the specific location because one of the Italian plants was an unsucessful pilot (see LIFE12 ENV/IT/000904 project, and supranational geographies do not work well with regionalized Life Cycle Impact Assessment Methods.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription:
This process represent the recycling of crystalline silicon photovoltaic modules using average technology for Europe from the calendar years 2015-2016).
It is based on mechnanical recycling operating in batches.
processDocumentation.modelingConstantsDescription: Latunussa et al. (2016) assumed a transport distance of 100 km by small truck to the collection point, and 400 km by an average truck to the the recycling site. These assumptions were also used by Frischknecht et al. (2020).
processDocumentation.completenessDescription:
Treatment of Missing Environmental Data

Wambach et al. (2017) indicated direct emissions, dust and water were not accounted for.

Treatment of Missing Technosphere

Wambach et al. (2017) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered, other than post procesing of the recycled output materials.

Mass Balance

No mass balance was calculated for this process.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: This unit process was modeled based on Frischknecht et al. (2020) inventory for the take back and rcyling of used crystalline silicon photovoltaic modules according to the end-of-life approach available on Table 30 of said report.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription:
The values in the LCI in Frischknecht et al. (2020) and Stoltz et al. (2017) represent a weighted average of five European recyclers.

Process data quality
Based on the information included in the “Data completeness section”, the data quality characteristic (DQC) "Process completeness" was given a score of 2 (60-79 % of determined flow have been evaluated and given a value). It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.
processDocumentation.samplingDescription:
System boundary conditions

The system boundary is cradle to grave and includes energy use, transport, and waste treatment. 

Data collection

The inventory present in Stoltz et al. (2017) was based on the responses to a questionnaire received from five European producers. 
Uncertainty
Frischknecht et al. (2020) included a standard deviation for all exchanges, which is repeated here.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: According to Wambach et al. (2017), recyclers were contacted between the calendar years 2015 and 2016, which is taken to be the data collection period.

sources.name: Latunussa et al. (2016) LCA recycling c-Si
Stolz et al. (2017) LCA PV recycling
Wambach et al. (2017) LCI PV recycling

reviews.details: t is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.

processDocumentation.intendedApplication:
Situation C2 - Accounting, without system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system excluding interactions with other systems in the LCI model.
The target audience of this model are LCA practitioners, the silicon, solar and related industries and the general public. The inventory is considered largely complete, except for land transformation activities. For that reason, it is not recommended to use this dataset when evaluating land use impact categories. The results of the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s data gap analysis were analyzed using the ISO 21930-LCIA-US impact assessment methodology, as well as Cumulative Energy Demand and a subset of ReCiPe 2016 impact categories.
The original application of this dataset was the identification of data gaps in the Federal LCA Commons (FLCAC) (see ""Project"" above). However, the intended application is to provide a cradle-to-gate inventory (A1-A3, according to ISO 21930) using exclusively processes currently available in USLCI or generated as part of the project mentioned above, and elementary flows from the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) to enable the use of a fuller range of LCIA impact categories than those reported in EPDs. For data quality purposes, the intended application is to provide a US average inventory for the year of publication of the original source.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed as part of the “Public Life Cycle Inventory Data Gap Analysis for Silicon-based Residential Photovoltaic Systems” National Institute of Standards and Technology’s Technical Note.

dataSetOwner.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
